name: User drafts a second quote in the same conversation; both action cards live in the thread
description: After locking + sending Quote A, user types another job description in the same thread. The handler should NOT update Quote A in-place (it's already 'sent'); instead it creates Quote B as a fresh draft, binds conv.quoteId to B, and renders a new action_card. The thread's preview reflects whichever quote was last interacted with.

persona.name: Diego
persona.role: Multi-tasking — quoting two jobs back-to-back from the same chat
persona.context: Easier to use one thread than spawn a new conversation for every job.

preconditions: Conversation has Quote A (status='sent', summary='Patel fence repair', $430)
Same thread, no new message yet

steps.action: Sends 'New quote — Hernandez deck stain, $620 labor + $180 materials'
steps.expect: create_quote action_card for Quote B in the same thread; conv.quoteId now points to Quote B; Quote A's card is unchanged earlier in the scroll
lock_quote fires for Quote B (NOT A); Quote B status flips to 'sent'; email queued to Hernandez customer

steps.action: Re-opens Quote A's card and clicks 'Re-open' (from the sent action_card)
steps.expect: Conversation continues; LLM is told 'reopen the patel quote'; conv.quoteId rebinds to Quote A so further edits target it

evaluationCriteria.check: Both action_cards are visually distinct in the scroll; the second one isn't accidentally rendered as a duplicate of the first
User isn't forced to start a new conversation for each job — the thread is conversational, not transactional
QuoteStore creates a new Quote row for B (not update-in-place on A); conv.quoteId rebinds correctly on each create_quote
Thread scroll preserves position when new card lands at the bottom; user isn't yanked away from the previous card
